title: Rich Briny Greek Fish Galaktoboureko
description: A briny and tender Greek dish featuring tarragon leaves and fish, shaped like nigiri and baked in a terracotta dish. This pescatarian-friendly galaktoboureko is a quick fix for a delicious meal.
instructions: Season the cod fillets with salt and pepper.
Mix the chopped tarragon leaves with crumbly feta cheese and pitted kalamata olives.
Place a spoonful of the tarragon mixture onto the center of each cod fillet.
Brush the edges of the phyllo dough sheets with olive oil and place a cod fillet in the center of each sheet.
Fold the phyllo dough over the fish to form a triangle or rectangle shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the galaktoboureko in a terracotta dish and brush the tops with lemon juice.
Bake the galaktoboureko for 20 minutes or until the phyllo dough is golden brown.
Serve the galaktoboureko hot and enjoy.
